examples: The accusation of incuriosity is one that we hear often, carrying the suggestion that there is something wrong with not wanting to search out the truth.
Previously he had been inclined to detach himself from the plight of victims of tragedy, not out of callousness but a sort of incuriosity.
On the matter of Mr. Obama's school records, locked away and secured against investigation, the press maintains a serene incuriosity.
